page:
# Day 9: Marine Mammals
## Focus
Cetaceans, pinnipeds, sea otters, thermoregulation, communication, migration, and social behavior.
## Learning Objectives
- Compare major marine mammal groups and their adaptations. - Explain how marine mammals conserve heat and navigate large habitats. - Connect behavior to ecological role and conservation needs.
## Core Topics
### Cetaceans
Whales, dolphins, and porpoises show strong adaptations for swimming, diving, communication, and, in many species, echolocation.
### Pinnipeds and Sea Otters
Marine mammals use blubber, dense fur, body size, and behavior to reduce heat loss in water.
### Migration and Social Structure
Many species rely on migration corridors, breeding grounds, acoustic communication, and social learning.
## Applied Activity
Prepare for the group presentation by selecting a marine ecosystem and identifying:
- one flagship species - one ecological interaction - one major conservation challenge
## Discussion Prompt
How do marine mammals illustrate the connection between physiology, behavior, and habitat protection?
